_notes: NFL scheduling constraints derived from the NFL Constitution, CBA (2020), and broadcast contracts.
Hard constraints are inviolable; soft constraints carry weighted penalties.
The NFL schedule is not round-robin: fixture generation uses a rotation formula.
Sources: NFL Competition Committee rulebook, NFLPA CBA 2020, Recentive Analytics public statements,
ESPN/NBC/CBS/Amazon/Fox broadcast contracts, NFL International Series operations manual.

hard.source: NFL Constitution Art. XVII; 17-game regular season effective 2021
hard.description: Each team plays exactly 17 regular-season games across 18 weeks (one bye week)

hard.source: NFL scheduling formula — all 6 intra-division games are mandatory and fixed
hard.description: Each team plays home and away against each of its 3 division rivals (6 games)
4 games vs one other division within same conference (home vs all or away vs all), rotating on a 3-year cycle
hard.source: NFL scheduling formula — intra-conference cross-division rotation

hard.description: 4 games vs one division from the opposite conference (home/away split), rotating on a 4-year cycle
hard.source: NFL scheduling formula — inter-conference cross-division rotation

hard.description: 2 games vs teams from remaining two same-conference divisions that finished in the same place in standings the prior year
1 game vs team from the opposite conference division that mirrors the team's own inter-conference rotation, matched by same prior-year division finish

hard.source: NFLPA CBA 2020 Art. 27 — mandatory bye weeks between weeks 6-14 inclusive
hard.description: Every team has exactly one bye week; scheduling window is weeks 6-14

hard.source: Stadium operations — shared stadiums cannot host both tenants on the same day
hard.description: Teams sharing a stadium cannot both play home games on the same date

hard.source: NFL tradition codified in broadcast contracts (CBS/Fox) — since 1966 (DET) and 1978 (DAL)
hard.description: Dallas Cowboys and Detroit Lions must play home on Thanksgiving Day; a third evening game is also scheduled (host varies)

hard.source: NFLPA CBA 2020 — health & safety; minimum rest before Thursday Night Football
hard.description: Teams playing TNF must have at least 10 days since their previous game (exception: season-opening Thursday game)

hard.source: NFL operational rule — teams cannot play on Dec 24 and Dec 25 consecutively
hard.description: No team may play a game on Christmas Eve (Dec 24) and Christmas Day (Dec 25) in the same season

hard.source: NFL Constitution — 18-week regular season calendar with one bye per team
hard.description: The regular season spans exactly 18 calendar weeks from opening weekend to Week 18 finale

soft.source: NFL scheduling preference — player welfare, home attendance, fan engagement
soft.description: Teams should not play more than 3 consecutive road games

soft.source: NFL scheduling preference — competitive balance and broadcast variety
soft.description: Teams should not play more than 4 consecutive home games

soft.source: NFLPA CBA 2020 — short-week rest; teams on TNF after Sunday road game get only 3 days rest
soft.description: Strongly avoid scheduling a team on Thursday Night Football the week after a Sunday road game (only 3 days rest)

soft.source: NFL broadcast commitment — all 32 teams should appear in at least one prime-time national window
soft.description: Every team should appear in at least one Sunday Night Football, Monday Night Football, or Thursday Night Football broadcast

soft.source: NFL broadcast distribution — cap on primetime concentration to maintain variety for fans
soft.description: No team should appear in more than 8 prime-time windows per season (prevents primetime saturation)

soft.source: NFL Competition Committee — scheduling formula is designed so division rivals face identical opponents in rotation games
soft.description: Teams in the same division face opponents of equivalent prior-year strength in rotation slots

soft.source: NFLPA CBA / team operations — minimize consecutive coast-to-coast trips (3+ time zone crossings)
soft.description: Minimize consecutive long-haul road trips (e.g. East Coast team to West Coast back-to-back weeks)

soft.source: NFL scheduling standard — competitive balance; teams should not front-load or back-load home games
soft.description: Teams should have a balanced home/away split across the first half (weeks 1-9) and second half (weeks 10-18) of the season

soft.source: NFL International Series operations — London, Munich, São Paulo games require extra preparation and recovery
soft.description: Teams playing international games (London, Munich, Brazil) should have at least 7 days rest before and after the game

soft.source: NBC/NFL broadcast agreement — SNF flex scheduling requires viable matchup options in late-season weeks 5-18
soft.description: Each week from 5-18 should include at least one flex-eligible game (competitive teams) so NBC can promote the best matchup to SNF

soft.source: NFL scheduling preference — spread division games across the season for sustained rivalry interest
soft.description: The home and away legs of each division rivalry should be at least 5 weeks apart

soft.source: NFL operations — Thanksgiving night game (non-DAL/DET) should rotate among teams that haven't hosted recently
soft.description: The third Thanksgiving game host (prime-time slot) should rotate; teams shouldn't host consecutive years unless they are DAL or DET
